classObjective: Solve one-step inequalities using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, including when the coefficient is negative.
workedExamples: 6-1, Example 3 — Solve and Graph Inequalities Using Addition and Subtraction
6-1, Example 4 — Solve and Graph Inequalities Using Multiplication and Division
6-1, Example 5 — Apply Inequalities to Real-World Problems
6-1, Example 6 — Solve and Graph Inequalities (Multiplication and Division with Negatives)
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: reverse inequality symbol when dividing/multiplying by negatives; checking solution sets
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice solving one-step inequalities including negative coefficient cases
independentPractice: Practice problems solving one-step inequalities using all four operations
assessment: Exit ticket: solve -3x >= 12 and graph the solution set
capReflection: Why does the inequality sign reverse when you multiply or divide by a negative number?
